name: Applesauce Treasure Loaf
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Quaker Oats, uncooked (quick or old-fashioned)
1 cup Pitted dates (cut in small pieces)
1 cup Candied red cherries (cut in small pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat shortening and sugar together until creamy.
2. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
3. Sift together 2 cups of the flour, baking powder, soda, salt, and spices.
4. Add alternately with applesauce to the creamed mixture; blend well.
5. Stir in oats.
6. Combine remaining 1/2 cup flour with raisins, dates, cherries, and nutmeats; stir to coat fruits and nutmeats evenly with flour.
7. Stir into batter.
8. Pour into 2 greased and waxed paper-lined 8-1/2 inches x 4-1/2 inches x 2-1/2 inches loaf pans.
9. Bake in a preheated very slow oven (275°F) about 2 hours.
10. Set pan of hot water in the bottom of the oven while loaves are baking.
11. Loosen edges; cool on wire rack about 10 minutes.
12. Remove from pans; cool thoroughly.
13. Wrap cooled bread and store for one day before slicing.
